2026-04-13T14:43:11.5232240Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:11.5232622Z ---
2026-04-13T14:43:11.5233117Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:11.5239750Z **Analysis Comment (would have been posted):**
2026-04-13T14:43:11.5240392Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:11.5245254Z ## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage
2026-04-13T14:43:11.5245787Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:12.6446639Z **Summary:** `Azure.Storage.Blobs` 12.27.0 blob uploads fail with HTTP 400 when routed through a HTTPS forward proxy (CONNECT tunnel), while downloads through the same proxy succeed. The issue appears to be related to how the SDK streams request bodies over an established tunnel.
2026-04-13T14:43:12.6447789Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:13.8343372Z <details>
2026-04-13T14:43:13.8344112Z <summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>
2026-04-13T14:43:13.8344506Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:13.8361031Z **Package:** `Azure.Storage.Blobs` 12.27.0 on .NET 8.0.25 (Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS)
2026-04-13T14:43:15.0901621Z **Affected API:** `BlobClient.UploadAsync` / block upload pipeline
2026-04-13T14:43:15.0903535Z **Scenarios:**
2026-04-13T14:43:16.3151068Z - Blob upload via `Azure.Storage.Blobs` fails with HTTP 400 when `HTTPS_PROXY` points to a GCP Secure Web Proxy using a CONNECT tunnel
2026-04-13T14:43:16.3164725Z - Only ~17 KB is transferred in 75 seconds before the server returns 400
2026-04-13T14:43:16.3170245Z - Downloads through the same proxy and tunnel succeed without issue
2026-04-13T14:43:16.3170991Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:17.5187624Z **Root ask:** Blob uploads should work through HTTPS forward proxies that establish CONNECT tunnels, consistent with how downloads work.
2026-04-13T14:43:17.5189947Z </details>
2026-04-13T14:43:17.5190549Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:17.5190972Z <details>
2026-04-13T14:43:17.5192470Z <summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>
2026-04-13T14:43:17.5193766Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:18.6837153Z The asymmetry between upload failure and download success through the same proxy tunnel is informative. Key observations:
2026-04-13T14:43:18.6841730Z - The CONNECT tunnel is established successfully (HTTP 200 from proxy)
2026-04-13T14:43:18.6845719Z - TLS handshake completes within the tunnel
2026-04-13T14:43:19.8989436Z - Very small amount of data sent (17 KB) over an unusually long time (75s) suggests a flow control or chunk encoding mismatch
2026-04-13T14:43:19.9035686Z - The 400 Bad Request comes from Azure Blob Storage, not the proxy (proxy doesn't inspect TLS traffic)
2026-04-13T14:43:21.2263095Z - Similar issues have been traced to `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` vs `Content-Length` differences in request handling, or to how the SDK buffers/streams upload data over keep-alive connections
2026-04-13T14:43:21.2264293Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:21.2296467Z **Suggested investigation steps:**
2026-04-13T14:43:22.3023424Z 1. Capture a Fiddler/Wireshark trace (or enable `AZURE_LOG_LEVEL=verbose`) to inspect the actual HTTP headers sent during the failed upload — particularly `Transfer-Encoding`, `Content-Length`, and whether the request body is being sent chunked or buffered
2026-04-13T14:43:23.5077875Z 2. Compare HTTP traffic characteristics between the upload failure and a direct (no-proxy) upload to see if proxy-induced changes to the stream affect request framing
2026-04-13T14:43:24.6151853Z 3. Test with `StorageTransferOptions` to force different upload strategies (e.g., `InitialTransferSize`, `MaximumTransferSize`) — if smaller blocks succeed it points to a streaming/chunking issue
2026-04-13T14:43:25.9406132Z 4. Check if the issue reproduces with `Azure.Storage.Blobs` 12.x without the proxy to rule out server-side 400 causes unrelated to the proxy
2026-04-13T14:43:25.9407232Z </details>
2026-04-13T14:43:25.9407428Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:25.9407576Z <details>
2026-04-13T14:43:25.9414715Z <summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>
2026-04-13T14:43:25.9415066Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:27.1009060Z - **Category:** `Client` — The issue is clearly in the SDK's HTTP upload behavior (chunked transfer, proxy handling), not in the Azure service REST API itself
2026-04-13T14:43:27.1019183Z - **Service:** `Storage` — Package `Azure.Storage.Blobs` is under `/sdk/storage`
2026-04-13T14:43:28.3532840Z - **Confidence:** High — Package explicitly named, unambiguous mapping to Storage/Client; no alternative services or categories considered
2026-04-13T14:43:28.3534289Z </details>
2026-04-13T14:43:28.3534533Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:28.3534669Z <details>
2026-04-13T14:43:28.3536835Z <summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>
2026-04-13T14:43:28.3537376Z 
2026-04-13T14:43:28.3548782Z - **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Storage` (line 493) — only requires "Storage" label which is present
2026-04-13T14:43:29.4978494Z - **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
2026-04-13T14:43:29.4985603Z - **ServiceOwners:** @amnguye @jalauzon-msft @jaschrep-msft @nickliu-msft @seanmcc-msft
2026-04-13T14:43:29.5035288Z - **Routing action:** Added `Service Attention` + `needs-team-attention` labels; issue left unassigned; ServiceOwners mentioned in routing comment
2026-04-13T14:43:30.7003764Z - **Scan notes:** Line 683 `# ServiceLabel: %Data Lake Storage Gen2` encountered first (bottom-to-top) but requires "Data Lake Storage Gen2" label — not a match; line 493 `# ServiceLabel: %Storage` matches on "Storage" label
2026-04-13T14:43:32.3464144Z </details>
2026-04-13T14:43:32.3464403Z

Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment.
